alkylation reagent: alkylated with 10 mM chloroacetamide for 30 min at 37 °C
alkylation concentration: alkylated with 10 mM chloroacetamide for 30 min at 37 °C
cleavage agent: digested by incubation with sequencing-grade modified trypsin, sequencing-grade Glu-C, and sequencing-grade chymotrypsin
collision energy: The normalized collision energy was set to 28%
fragmentation method: followed by high-collision-dissociation (HCD) of the 20 most abundant precursor ions
instrument: Mass spectrometry analysis was performed on Q-Exactive HF equipped with a nanoelectrospray ion source
ionization type: equipped with a nanoelectrospray ion source
labeling: we employ liquid chromatography–mass spectrometry (LC-MS) to validate these findings
reduction reagent: the protein was reduced with 5 mM TCEP for 60 min at 37 °C
reduction concentration: the protein was reduced with 5 mM TCEP for 60 min at 37 °C
